Two high-value phishing intelligence feeds: (1) URLscan.io — free API, search for phishing pages by institution brand keywords, detects live phishing pages in real time. Poll periodically for each institution name. (2) PhishTank — community-submitted confirmed phishing URLs, free API. Both feeds should create mentions when a URL matches a monitored institution, and auto-create findings for confirmed phishing pages. Implementation: new connector classes, REST API polling, institution name matching.
title: Add URLscan.io and PhishTank feed connectors

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-pgjn
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-11T19:19:09Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Monitor known ransomware group .onion blogs (LockBit, ALPHV/BlackCat, Cl0p, Play, Royal, Akira, etc). Scrape victim listings and match against monitored institutions. URLs rotate frequently - store in source.config with last_known_url. Parse victim name, post date, data volume claims, countdown timers. Via Tor SOCKS proxy.
notes: Implemented RansomwareBlogConnector in src/darkdisco/discovery/connectors/ransomware_blog.py. Supports 10 ransomware groups (LockBit, ALPHV, Cl0p, Play, Royal, Akira, BianLian, Medusa, Rhysida, Hunters Intl). Generic HTML parser extracts victim names, dates, data volumes, countdown timers. Dedup via content hashing. Tor SOCKS proxy via aiohttp-socks. URL rotation with primary+mirror support.
title: Implement ransomware blog monitor - detect victim listings on ransomware group sites

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-mbwi
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-12T22:40:36Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Zero legitimate findings so far. The pipeline needs tuning to produce real results:
1. Verify watchterm matching is working correctly against live data coming from enabled connectors
2. Check if the matching logic in run_matching() is too strict or too loose
3. Review the paste site and forum data actually being collected - are the right sources/pages being scraped?
4. Ensure the Telegram stealer logs connector is ingesting credential data and matching against institution domains
5. Add debug logging or a report endpoint to show what raw mentions are being collected vs what passes matching
6. Consider adding more institutions/watchterms if coverage is too narrow

This is the critical path - the system has no value until it produces at least one real finding.
notes:
Findings and changes implemented:

1. FP filtering was too aggressive (threshold 0.70, auto-dismiss 0.90):
   - Raised to 0.80/0.95 respectively
   - institution_name matches were being penalized as "generic" — removed from generic_types
   - Single legitimate context indicator (e.g. "job posting") was suppressing real findings — now requires 2+
   - generic_short_term weight lowered from 0.6 to 0.4

2. Matcher used naive substring matching:
   - Domains now use boundary-aware regex (prevents "chase" in "purchased")
   - Institution names use \b word boundaries (prevents "first" in "unfirst")
   - BIN ranges and regex already used proper patterns

3. No pipeline diagnostics existed:
   - Added GET /pipeline/status — source health, watch term coverage, finding counts
   - Added POST /pipeline/dry-run — test matching without creating findings
   - Added detailed logging in run_matching: watch term types, per-mention match results, match-through rates

4. All 68 tests pass (enrichment + pipeline tests updated for new behavior)
title: Achieve first legitimate finding - tune matching and verify pipeline end-to-end

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-zow0
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-12T21:05:15Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Darkdisco acts as a client of trapline SaaS. Create a connector that: 1) Syncs all active institution domains, names, and BINs to trapline via POST /api/v1/watchlist/domains and /bins endpoints. Runs periodically to keep in sync. 2) Receives phishing findings from trapline via incoming webhook at POST /api/webhooks/trapline. Parses the finding payload, matches to institution, creates a darkdisco Finding with source_type=trapline. 3) Config: trapline_api_url, trapline_api_key, webhook_secret for HMAC verification. This makes darkdisco a consumer of trapline phishing intelligence for its monitored financial institutions.
title: Add trapline connector - sync institutions as watchlist and ingest phishing findings

API endpoint GET /mentions/channels already exists returning channel names with counts. (2) Single files (not archives) attached to mentions should be viewable directly in the UI — show text content inline for text files, image preview for images, and always offer a download link. Currently only archive contents are browsable. (3) Always show a download button for files regardless of whether we can determine the type — users should be able to download any file attached to a mention.

description:

DarkDisco should register its monitored institutions with trapline's client watchlist API so trapline can detect phishing targeting those institutions. Implementation: (1) add trapline_api_key and trapline_api_url to darkdisco config, (2) create a sync task that maps institutions to trapline watchlist entries — primary_domain + additional_domains as type=domain, institution name as type=brand, bin_ranges as WatchlistBin entries, (3) call POST /api/client/watchlist/domains and /bins on trapline, (4) run sync on institution create/update and periodically. Trapline's client watchlist API already exists and scores +15 points in triage for matches.
title: Sync institutions to trapline watchlist as client

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-onuda
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-18T01:59:04Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Findings created from trapline's webhook integration currently only contain the domain name. They should include the full intelligence context from trapline: (1) DNS records (A, CNAME, MX, NS) and resolved IPs with ASN info, (2) WHOIS data (registrar, creation date, expiry, name servers), (3) TLS certificate info (issuer, validity, SANs), (4) Browsing session screenshot if one was captured — embed or link the screenshot image in the finding detail, (5) Network log summary from the browsing session (contacted domains, resource types), (6) Score breakdown showing which signals triggered. The trapline webhook payload (finding.completed event) should include all this data. The darkdisco webhook receiver should store it in finding metadata and display it on the finding detail page.
title: Enrich trapline-sourced findings with DNS, WHOIS, TLS, and screenshots

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-7x41
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-12T21:18:28Z
dispatched_by: mayor

All 100 institutions have empty bin_ranges and routing_numbers arrays. Use the darkdisco postgres database directly to populate these. Connect via: docker compose exec postgres psql -U darkdisco. UPDATE institutions SET bin_ranges, routing_numbers for each institution. Use well-known public data: major credit unions and banks have published routing numbers. BIN ranges are the first 6 digits of card numbers issued by each institution. Populate at minimum the routing_number for each institution, and BIN prefixes where known. Also create watch_terms entries of type routing_number and bin_range for each populated value. This is critical for the matching pipeline to detect these in stealer logs and breach data.
notes: Findings: Data verified populated in app DB (port 5432). All 100 institutions have routing_numbers and bin_ranges. Watch terms: 100 routing_number + 105 bin_range entries created. Code change in seed_institutions.py updates _ensure_institution to handle idempotent re-runs.
title: Populate BIN ranges and routing numbers for all 100 institutions via database

NOTE: Trapline already has certstream for broad phishing detection — this connector should focus specifically on institution-targeted domains and cross-reference with the darkdisco institution list.

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-l8om1
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-14T22:55:00Z
dispatched_by: mayor

New channels have been joined but we need to verify: (1) all channels are in the Telegram Monitor source config, (2) the download_pending_files task is running and processing new files from all channels, (3) files from new channels get extracted via stream_extract_from_s3 after download, (4) the download beat schedule (every 10min) is active. Check worker logs to confirm polling picks up new channel messages with files.
notes:
Two bugs found and fixed in telegram.py:
1. _message_to_mention dropped messages with file attachments but no text/caption (line 295: 'if not text: return None'). Fixed to check 'if not text and not msg.file'.
2. File data was never downloaded - connector recorded metadata (file_name, file_size) but never called download_media(). Added download_media() call in _poll_channel with configurable max_file_size (default 50MB).
The beat schedule runs at 5min intervals via schedule_polls. No download_pending_files task exists - file processing is inline via _process_file_mentions during poll_source. stream_extract_from_s3 handles large archives via backfill_stored_archives task.
title: Ensure file download pipeline processes all channels with attachments

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-jeqpz
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-14T01:55:29Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Create a proper ExtractedFile model to normalize archive contents out of JSONB metadata. Table: mention_id (FK), filename, s3_key, sha256, size, extension, is_text bool, text_content (Text, nullable — populated for text files from S3 on extraction). Add PostgreSQL full-text search via tsvector/tsquery with GIN index on text_content for keyword search across all extracted files. Populate on archive extraction in _process_file_mentions and _download_files_async. Backfill existing mentions that have file_analysis in metadata.
notes: Implementation complete. Added ExtractedFile model (mention_id FK to raw_mentions, filename, s3_key, sha256, size, extension, is_text, text_content with tsvector/GIN FTS). Migration 003. Pipeline now persists RawMention model rows + ExtractedFile rows during run_matching. API archive-contents endpoints query ExtractedFile first with FTS, fallback to legacy JSONB. New /extracted-files/search endpoint. Backfill task chains batches. _download_files_async for S3 retrieval.
title: ExtractedFile table and file-level content storage

This lets analysts manually review ingested data and spot things the automated matching missed. Show: source, timestamp, content preview, and option to manually create a finding from a mention.
2. FINDING CONTENT IN-APP: The finding detail view should show full message content inline (especially for Telegram messages) rather than just linking to external sources. Include surrounding context where available - for Telegram this means showing the channel name, message timestamp, any media/file references, and forwarded-from info. Keep the external link as an option but make in-app review the primary workflow.

These are critical for analyst workflow - the system needs to surface ALL collected data, not just matched findings.

description: Discord connector code exists but no bot is configured. Steps: (1) user creates Discord bot at discord.com/developers with MESSAGE_CONTENT intent, (2) set DARKDISCO_DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN in .env, (3) create discord source in DB, (4) join relevant servers (phishing development, carding/fraud with phishing tool channels, tool development). Discord is secondary to Telegram for this threat space but still valuable. User will handle server joining; system needs the bot infrastructure ready.
title: Set up Discord bot and join fraud/phishing servers

description:
Actors frequently post PHOTOS OF SCREENS (not just screenshots) showing victim bank account dashboards, phishing panel results, and OTP interception successes. These images are often taken at angles, rotated, skewed, with poor lighting and varying quality. Standard Tesseract OCR will fail on these.

Implementation requirements:
(1) Use a robust OCR pipeline that handles perspective distortion, rotation, and skew. Options: EasyOCR (handles rotated text natively), PaddleOCR (best accuracy on distorted text), or Google Vision API. EasyOCR is the best self-hosted option — it uses deep learning models that handle arbitrary text orientations.
(2) Pre-processing pipeline: auto-rotate, deskew, contrast enhancement, perspective correction before OCR. OpenCV can handle this.
(3) Download images from Telegram messages (photos, not just file attachments — actors use inline photos extensively).
(4) Run OCR on all images to extract text.
(5) Feed OCR text through the existing watch term matcher — match institution names, BINs, routing numbers, account numbers.
(6) Store OCR text in mention metadata for searchability.
(7) Include OCR-extracted details (names, partial account numbers, institution branding) in findings that get pushed to institutions via trapline webhook.
(8) Handle both MessageMediaPhoto (inline photos) and MessageMediaDocument (image files) from Telegram.

Priority: This captures the highest-value intelligence — visual proof of compromised accounts with identifying details that institutions need for customer notification.
title: OCR processing for screenshot/image analysis in Telegram messages

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-a1zyy
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-18T02:17:25Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Enhance the finding review workflow with:

(1) EDITABLE SEVERITY: The reviewer should be able to change the severity (critical/high/medium/low/info) from the finding detail page. Currently severity is set at creation and cannot be changed.

(2) NATURE/CLASSIFICATION LABEL: A new field 'classification' (varchar) that captures the nature of the finding in 1-2 words. UI: a small text input combined with a dropdown that shows previously used classifications as suggestions (autocomplete from distinct values). Examples: 'account takeover', 'credential theft', 'phishing kit', 'scam page', 'wire fraud', 'OTP interception', 'data leak'.

(3) ANALYST NOTES: A text area for in-depth comments by the reviewer. Append-only (new notes added with timestamp, not replacing previous notes). Display as a chronological thread.

(4) AUDIT LOG: Record all review actions with timestamp, user, action type, and before/after values. Actions to log: status change, severity change, classification set/changed, notes added, finding dismissed/confirmed. Display audit history on the finding detail page.

Add DB columns: classification (varchar), reviewed_by (varchar), reviewed_at (timestamp). The analyst_notes field already exists but needs the append-only thread behavior.
title: Finding review enhancements: editable severity, nature label, analyst notes, audit log

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-w4pl
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-12T21:10:51Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Multiple issues with the sources page need fixing:

1. HEALTH INCONSISTENCY: Telegram Stealer Logs shows healthy on overview cards but offline in detail view. The health computation needs to be consistent between list and detail endpoints.

2. ENABLE/DISABLE TOGGLE: Each source card and detail view needs a toggle switch to enable/disable the source. Wire to PUT /sources/{id} with enabled field. Currently 5 sources enabled and 11 disabled - users need to manage this from the UI.

3. DEGRADED STATUS DETAILS: When a source is degraded or has errors, the detail view should prominently show last_error, last_polled_at, and poll_interval_seconds so the user understands why. Show error messages in a dismissable alert banner.

4. DISABLED SOURCE VISIBILITY: Disabled sources should still appear in the overview but visually distinct (greyed out or marked). Currently unclear if disabled sources show at all.

5. Source list should show enabled/disabled filter toggle.

Frontend files: Sources.tsx, SourceDetail.tsx, api.ts, types.ts
Backend: GET /sources endpoint optionally filters by enabled, GET /sources/{id} needs same health computation as list endpoint.
title: Sources page overhaul - health consistency, enable/disable toggle, degraded status details

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-rf65i
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-17T22:04:43Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Build tests covering every regression encountered during the March 13-17 session:

API/AUTH TESTS:
- Login returns valid JWT
- Finding status transitions work for all valid transitions (new→reviewing, new→confirmed, new→dismissed, etc.)
- Watch term creation with all WatchTermType enum values (institution_name, domain, bin_range, keyword, regex)
- Archive contents endpoint returns files (fallback to S3 listing when no ExtractedFile rows)
- Extracted files search endpoint returns results with archive_name grouping

TELEGRAM TESTS:
- Connector poll returns mentions from joined channels
- High-water marks persist to source config after poll
- Time filter skipped for channels with no high-water mark (backfill mode)
- extract_channel_links extracts t.me/ URLs from message text
- download_media method exists on TelegramConnector and accepts message_id + channel_id
- Session lock serializes poll and download tasks (no SQLite contention)

DATA MODEL TESTS:
- RawMention uses metadata_ (not metadata) for JSONB column access
- mention.metadata (dataclass) vs mention.metadata_ (SQLAlchemy model) distinction
- DiscoveredChannel model and API endpoints work
- ExtractedFile table exists with FTS index
- FindingStatus enum includes confirmed and dismissed
- content_hash dedup prevents duplicate raw_mentions

PIPELINE TESTS:
- _process_file_mentions accesses mention.metadata (dataclass), not metadata_
- run_matching creates findings for institution name matches
- Watchlist sync to trapline uses X-API-Key header and correct endpoint paths
- Watchlist sync extracts short brand names from institution names
- OCR module exists and extract_text_from_image function is callable

FILE HANDLING TESTS:
- is_archive handles None filename
- Download task marks files as stored/error with correct metadata updates
- S3 file listing returns files with preserved archive paths
- Archive contents API returns file list from file_analysis metadata fallback

FRONTEND TESTS:
- termTypes array matches WatchTermType enum values
- highlightText handles null/undefined text input
- Mentions page pagination state exists
- Channel filter sends channel parameter to API
title: Comprehensive test suite for regression prevention

Two items: (1) IMAGE DEDUP: Before running OCR, hash the image data (SHA256 or perceptual hash like pHash) and skip processing if the same image was already OCR'd. Actors frequently repost the same screenshots across channels and in repeated messages. Store image hashes in mention metadata or a separate table. (2) HISTORICAL OCR BACKFILL: Run OCR on all images from the last 30 days of messages. Create a backfill task that: finds mentions with image attachments (MessageMediaPhoto or image file extensions), downloads from S3, runs EasyOCR, appends OCR text to mention content, re-runs watch term matching on mentions with OCR text. Rate limit to avoid overloading the worker.
title: Image dedup before OCR and historical OCR backfill
